title: Knysna acts on livestock crisis
description: KNYSNA NEWS - Knysna Municipality has called...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"KNYSNA NEWS - Knysna Municipality has called an urgent meeting with local farmers, Sanral, the Department of Rural Development and Land Reform, the Department of Forestry, and other key stakeholders.","html":"KNYSNA NEWS - Knysna Municipality has called an urgent meeting with local farmers, Sanral, the Department of Rural Development and Land Reform, the Department of Forestry, and other key stakeholders."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"This follows an accident on the N2 near Nekkies on Tuesday 29 July, when a vehicle collided with a cow that was crossing the highway."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The incident has once again highlighted the growing danger posed by stray livestock on major roads in the area, raising serious concerns about road safety and public well-being.","html":"The incident has once again highlighted the growing danger posed by stray livestock on major roads in the area, raising serious concerns about road safety and public well-being."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"A stakeholder engagement session will take place in the council chamber at 14:00 on Monday 4 August to find a co-ordinated and sustainable solution to the ongoing issue of unattended livestock, particularly cattle, roaming public roads and urban areas.","html":"A stakeholder engagement session will take place in the council chamber at 14:00 on Monday 4 August to find a co-ordinated and sustainable solution to the ongoing issue of unattended livestock, particularly cattle, roaming public roads and urban areas."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“One of the core challenges is that many local farmers lack access to proper land and infrastructure for managing their livestock,” said Knysna Mayor Thando Matika.","html":"“One of the core challenges is that many local farmers lack access to proper land and infrastructure for managing their livestock,” said Knysna Mayor Thando Matika."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“We are working with national departments to explore long-term support options that will benefit both farmers and the wider community.”","html":"“We are working with national departments to explore long-term support options that will benefit both farmers and the wider community.”"},{"type":"blockquote","text":"In addition to seeking collaborative solutions, the municipality will continue to enforce relevant by-laws to protect motorists and animals."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“This is about more than just enforcement. It’s about protecting lives, supporting our farmers, and building a safer, more sustainable future for everyone in Knysna,” added Matika.","html":"“This is about more than just enforcement. It’s about protecting lives, supporting our farmers, and building a safer, more sustainable future for everyone in Knysna,” added Matika."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
